behaviors.description: The keyword-frequency analyzer assigns the correct sentiment when the review contains only words from the configured positive and negative word lists.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is local scoring logic (token counting + threshold comparison) with no required I/O. A pure mock would not hide the core bug here because the bug is in the function itself, not infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Call the analyzer function directly with injected positive/negative dictionaries and thresholds; verify returned label for representative positive-only, negative-only, and neutral-count cases at threshold edges.

behaviors.description: The explanation analyzer produces reasoning that references specific phrases from the original review, not generic boilerplate.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real language-generation behavior (grounding to input phrases). Mocking the explainer can trivially pass while hiding real failures like generic output or missing citation of source phrases.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real explanation analyzer/model on fixed reviews and assert the explanation includes at least one exact or near-exact phrase from the review text (with a lexical overlap threshold) and fails if output is generic boilerplate.

behaviors.description: When a review contains mixed signals (both positive and negative keywords), the sentiment score reflects the configured tie-breaking rule.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic decision logic in the keyword analyzer. Real failure is in tie-break implementation, not external infra. In-process test can directly expose wrong branch behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke scoring with crafted token counts that produce ties and near-ties under multiple configured tie-break rules; assert exact sentiment label chosen for each rule.

behaviors.description: The explanation for a sarcastic review (e.g., "Great, another broken feature") correctly identifies the negative intent despite positive surface words.
behaviors.justification: Sarcasm interpretation depends on semantic inference from a real model and can vary across runs/models. A mock or rule stub would likely miss this real-world failure mode.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real explanation analyzer on a sarcasm-focused test set and check that explanations explicitly indicate negative intent and mention contradictory cues (positive surface phrase + negative context). Use repeated runs/tolerance to account for non-determinism.

behaviors.description: Both outputs (score and explanation) are returned together in a single response, with the explanation referencing the same sentiment direction as the score.
behaviors.justification: This is orchestration plus semantic consistency between deterministic score and generated explanation. Mocking explanation can hide real contradiction failures; must exercise real generator to catch drift/mismatch.
behaviors.test_description: Call the end-to-end service endpoint with real analyzers enabled; assert response contains both fields in one payload and that explanation sentiment direction matches score (e.g., positive score cannot have negative rationale language), using rule-based consistency checks.
